title: artemisia norvegica subsp. heterophylla
intro: Artemisia norvegica subsp. heterophylla, also known as Norwegian wormwood, is a subspecies of the artemisia norvegica plant, which is a flowering plant native to Northern Europe and Northern Asia. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. The health benefits of Norwegian wormwood include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, making it a valuable herb for maintaining overall health and well-being. Herbal preparations of Norwegian wormwood can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
